response:
<<Executive Flight Analysis>>
profit The executive has a total flight duration of eight hours.
profit They must allocate two hours to solving a complex math problem.
profit This leaves six hours available for reading the thriller.
profit The book contains four hundred eighty pages in total.
profit To finish the book in the remaining six hours, the reading speed must increase.
profit Dividing the total pages by the available time yields a required speed of eighty pages per hour.
profit The original speed of sixty pages per hour is insufficient for the new schedule.
profit The excitement level of the thriller grows at a rate of five percent per hour.
profit The initial excitement level is quantified as one hundred units.
profit The equation representing this growth is E(t) equals one hundred times one point zero five to the power of t.
profit Calculating the excitement after eight hours involves raising one point zero five to the eighth power.
profit The resulting value for the exponent is approximately one point forty seven seven.
profit Multiplying this factor by the initial one hundred units gives a final excitement level.
profit The excitement level after the eight-hour flight is approximately one hundred forty seven point seven units.
profit Both the reading speed adjustment and the excitement calculation are now complete.
